parameters.content:
# HMZ - Reply SLA Watchdog - Validation (Phase 4B)

WHAT: Periodically (Schedule Trigger, eventual) or on demand (Execute Workflow Trigger, synthetic tests) reads every unfinished hmz-send-state send/error record from GET /v1/unfinished, classifies each into a watchdog category (AI_CLASSIFICATION_WAIT, API_RETRY, HUMAN_REVIEW, SEND_FAILURE, SEND_UNCERTAIN, SUPPRESSION_FAILURE, UNKNOWN_STATE), and compares its age against the WARNING (180s) and BREACH (300s) thresholds.

SLOs: only SEND_UNCERTAIN records belong to the Transmission SLO (an attempted send awaiting reconciliation). Every other category - including every HUMAN_REVIEW item - belongs to the Processing SLO. A draft, queue item, approval request, or human-review item is never counted as transmitted.

ALERTING: warning/breach records are reduced to sanitised alert candidates (category + SLO type + record identifier + status) and deduplicated atomically via POST /v1/alert/dedupe before routing to a PLACEHOLDER_NOT_CONFIGURED notification object only - no real notification call.

VALIDATION MODE: hardcoded (watchdog_result.validation_mode = true). SIDECAR: http://hmz-send-state:5681 (internal Docker network only).

INACTIVE: this workflow must remain inactive after import and after every test.

## C-J. Evaluation, Alerting, Persistence

C merges GET /v1/unfinished (sends/errors) onto the triggering item, preserving any test_overrides.now / synthetic flag from the Execute Workflow Trigger so synthetic runs are deterministic. D classifies and ages every record (warning_threshold_seconds=180, breach_threshold_seconds=300) and splits Processing vs Transmission SLO totals.

E builds one sanitised alert candidate per warning/breach record (stable alert_key = category + SLO type + kind + identifier + status, no message bodies, no raw email addresses - identifiers are already sha256 hashes). F atomically records each alert_key at most once via POST /v1/alert/dedupe (open('wx')); G merges the dedupe response so a repeated alert is never re-notified.

H builds the final sanitised watchdog_result (validation_mode=true, notification.surface=PLACEHOLDER_NOT_CONFIGURED, delivered=false - no real notification call). I persists it via POST /v1/phase4b/result; J attaches the returned resultId.

nodes.name: G4. Skip Watchdog Notification (Not Configured / No Alerts)

main.node: G4. Skip Watchdog Notification (Not Configured / No Alerts)
